vehicle.label: 6-DOF Industrial Arm
vehicle.desc: 6-DOF serial industrial robot arm, 5kg payload, 600mm reach

parts.label: Base Joint (J1)
parts.spec: Base rotary joint J1, +-180deg, harmonic drive, 100Nm peak torque, mounting flange ISO 9409

parts.label: Shoulder Joint (J2)
parts.spec: Shoulder pitch joint J2, +-120deg, harmonic drive, 80Nm, cable routing through center bore

parts.label: Upper Arm (Link 2-3)
parts.spec: Upper arm link, 300mm length, hollow tube with cable conduit, lightweight Al 7075

parts.label: Elbow Joint (J3)
parts.spec: Elbow pitch joint J3, +-150deg, cycloidal reducer, 50Nm, encoder dual-stack

parts.label: Forearm + Wrist (J4)
parts.spec: Forearm with wrist roll J4, 250mm length, +-360deg continuous, slip ring for power/signal

parts.label: Wrist Pitch (J5)
parts.spec: Wrist pitch joint J5, +-120deg, harmonic drive, 20Nm, compact form factor

parts.label: Wrist Roll (J6)
parts.spec: Wrist final roll joint J6, +-360deg, harmonic drive, 10Nm, ISO 9409-1 50mm tool flange

parts.label: Tool Flange + Changer
parts.spec: Tool changer master plate at J6 end, pneumatic locking, 4 utility ports

parts.label: Cable Management Spine
parts.spec: External cable carrier from base to wrist, drag chain or spiral, signal + power + pneumatic

parts.label: Replaceable Motor Module Set
parts.spec: Actuation_motors closure module: replaceable motor_module cartridges for J1-J6, fastener_access, coupling_dummy, insert/washer preload pads, torque_Nm intent labels

parts.label: Encoder Cover + Sensing Set
parts.spec: Sensing_encoders closure module: encoder_pocket and encoder_cover at each joint, index marks, cable exit strain relief, inspection labels

parts.label: Drive Electronics Bay
parts.spec: Drive_electronics bay with driver_bay, power_channel, signal_channel, ground_point, service cover, connector keying, thermal inspection window

requirements: Base plate with mounting holes, leveling, and cable entry
Base rotary joint J1, +-180deg, harmonic drive, 100Nm peak torque, mounting flange ISO 9409
Vehicle context: 6-DOF serial industrial robot arm, 5kg payload, 600mm reach
Suggested material: Al 7075 + steel + composite, process: CNC + LPBF

risk.desc: Thermal management may be insufficient for high cycle operations.
risk.mit: Implement additional cooling solutions such as heat sinks or active cooling.

risk.desc: Potential for cable harness strain relief to be inadequate.
risk.mit: Ensure all cable exits have strain relief geometry and comply with minimum bend radius.

risk.desc: Mounting holes may not be adequately reinforced for high torque.
risk.mit: Increase the thickness of the base plate and reinforce mounting holes.

risk.desc: Harmonic drive interface may require additional alignment features.
risk.mit: Add alignment pins or dowels to ensure precise fit.

risk.desc: Service access may be limited due to tight integration.
risk.mit: Ensure all necessary access points are clearly marked and easily accessible.

generated_parts.label: Cable Management Spine
generated_parts.spec: External cable carrier from base to wrist, drag chain or spiral, signal + power + pneumatic

requirements: External cable carrier from base to wrist, drag chain or spiral, signal + power + pneumatic
Cable spine with slack loops at every joint
Connection types and load-path closure
Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels
Cables exit with strain relief geometry
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure
Joints show bearing seat with shoulder or retaining feature
Harness crossing each joint has slack loop and routing avoids pinch points
Interfaces name specific connection type with its DOF and clearance
Assembly sequence is feasible and stated
One primary datum part anchors the assembly
Every part's primary load traces through named interfaces to the base/ground
Design moves the right direction in structural development
Assembly uses the fewest interfaces that still allow service and manufacture
P0 internal_feature_targets appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets
Geometry_ops include args.at coordinates distributed across the local subsystem volume
Uses drill, pocket, boss, channel, pattern_polar, fillet, chamfer
Every part_tree child has 4+ dedicated geometry_ops
A primary datum feature is explicitly named and placed
Adjacent interfaces include fit_class and stack_up_mm
Assembly sequence is defined with tool, torque, datum per step
Uses seed-characteristic ops: chamfer, drill, subtract, pocket
Dimensions match robot_arm scale: 80-400mm per link, 600-1200mm reach
Budget rule: mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value
Critical joints need visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque_Nm or preload intent
Interfaces name exact neighboring subsystem labels when they mate
Training-quality rule: make part_tree children and geometry_ops mutually traceable by id/name
Analysis-ready rule: verify must include boundary_conditions object with fixture_points, thermal_zones, and pressure_faces
Assembly sequence rule: cad_brief must include assembly_sequence and disassembly_sequence for field-serviceable parts
Tolerance stack-up rule: for each adjacent_interface in the subsystem plan, include stack_up_mm and fit_class
Dimensional realism rule: all dimensions must be physically plausible for the subsystem scale
